summary: OAuth 'click does nothing' fix verified end-to-end. In State 2 (Cloud ENABLED with fake creds), clicking Google and Apple buttons now triggers an EXPLICIT browser navigation to https://exampletest.supabase.co/auth/v1/authorize?... — Playwright caught net::ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED (expected: fake domain), proving the redirect fires. State 1 (Cloud DISABLED) regression baseline is clean.

state2_cloud_enabled.boot_log: [Ledger] Cloud sync: ENABLED | url=exampletest | key=set (205 chars, prefix=eyJ…)

google_oauth.sdk_log: [Ledger] signInWithOAuth result: {provider: google, hasUrl: true, url: https://exampletest.supabase.co/auth/v1/authorize?…&code_challenge_method=s256&prompt=select_account, error: null}

email_password.toast_shown: Failed to fetch (expected for fake domain)

critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.jsx L41: redirectTo=window.location.origin correctly matches root-level Supabase allowlist. Good.
AuthContext.jsx L61: window.location.href=data.url ensures explicit navigation independent of SDK's internal assign timing. Fix is correct.
AuthDialog.jsx L28-31: !data?.url actionable toast in place — no silent failure path.

Minor: 'Local only' chip still shows in sidebar when cloudEnabled=true but user not signed in yet. Consistent with UX intent (sync inactive until signed in) but could be misleading — consider a 'Not signed in' label instead.
updated_files: /app/frontend/.env (temporarily modified for State 2 testing, then restored to empty Supabase vars)
context_for_next_testing_agent: OAuth navigation flow is confirmed working. Real Supabase creds cannot be tested here (fake domain gives ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED which is actually the proof of navigation). If user reports issues after providing real Supabase creds, focus on Site URL / Redirect URLs allowlist in Supabase dashboard.
